title: helleborine palustris f. longibracteata
intro: Helleborine palustris f. longibracteata is a rare, terrestrial orchid species known for its unique and delicate appearance, characterized by long, narrow leaves and small, white or pale yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. In terms of health benefits, the plant is believed to help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues, while also exhibiting antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. The plant is traditionally prepared as a tea or tincture, which is used to treat various health conditions, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
